classData.comment:
/**
 * Internal typed-parser registry for `Neo.ai.ConfigProvider`.
 *
 * Each parser reads `env[envVarName]` and decodes it to a typed value, answering exactly one
 * question: "env var absent (undefined) / decoded value / invalid (warn + undefined)". The
 * decoders know NOTHING about specific configs, defaults, or domain consumers.
 *
 * **Not consumer-facing.** These parsers are the env-decode layer that `ConfigProvider` wires into
 * its meta-leaf registry: a `leaf(default, 'NEO_X', type)` resolves its env override through the
 * `type`-keyed parser here. Application code does NOT call `Env.parseX` directly — it reads the
 * resolved value via the Provider-extending aiConfig substrate (`AiConfig.<path>`), which layers
 * the env override over the config default. See `learn/agentos/AiConfigModel.md`.
 *
 * Uses the gatekeep pattern (lightweight stateless utility — no Base inheritance, no
 * reactive configs, no lifecycle hooks), mirroring `src/core/IdGenerator.mjs` precedent.
 *
 * @namespace Neo.util.Env
 */
classData.description:
Internal typed-parser registry for Neo.ai.ConfigProvider.

Each parser reads env[envVarName] and decodes it to a typed value, answering exactly one question: "env var absent (undefined) / decoded value / invalid (warn + undefined)". The decoders know NOTHING about specific configs, defaults, or domain consumers.

Not consumer-facing. These parsers are the env-decode layer that ConfigProvider wires into its meta-leaf registry: a leaf(default, 'NEO_X', type) resolves its env override through the type-keyed parser here. Application code does NOT call Env.parseX directly — it reads the resolved value via the Provider-extending aiConfig substrate (AiConfig.<path>), which layers the env override over the config default. See learn/agentos/AiConfigModel.md.

Uses the gatekeep pattern (lightweight stateless utility — no Base inheritance, no reactive configs, no lifecycle hooks), mirroring src/core/IdGenerator.mjs precedent.
classData.name: Env

classData.comment:
/**
     * Tokens accepted as `true` by `parseBool` (case-insensitive after trim).
     * Covers both MCP (`'true'`) and daemon (`'yes'`/`'on'`/`'1'`) conventions.
     * @member {String[]} TRUE_TOKENS
     */
classData.description: Tokens accepted as true by parseBool (case-insensitive after trim). Covers both MCP ('true') and daemon ('yes'/'on'/'1') conventions.
classData.name: TRUE_TOKENS

classData.comment:
/**
     * Tokens accepted as `false` by `parseBool` (case-insensitive after trim).
     * Preserves `PrimaryRepoSyncService.parseEnabledFlag` legacy semantics
     * (`'0'` / `'false'` / `'no'` / `'off'` → false).
     * @member {String[]} FALSE_TOKENS
     */
classData.description: Tokens accepted as false by parseBool (case-insensitive after trim). Preserves PrimaryRepoSyncService.parseEnabledFlag legacy semantics ('0' / 'false' / 'no' / 'off' → false).
classData.name: FALSE_TOKENS

classData.comment:
/**
     * Decode env value as boolean.
     *
     * Token semantics (case-insensitive, trimmed):
     * - true:  `'true'`, `'yes'`, `'on'`, `'1'`
     * - false: `'false'`, `'no'`, `'off'`, `'0'`
     * - other: warn + undefined
     *
     * @param {String} envVarName
     * @param {Object} [opts]
     * @param {Object} [opts.env=process.env]
     * @param {Function} [opts.warn=console.warn]
     * @returns {Boolean|undefined}
     */
classData.description:
Decode env value as boolean.

Token semantics (case-insensitive, trimmed):

  • true: 'true', 'yes', 'on', '1'
  • false: 'false', 'no', 'off', '0'
  • other: warn + undefined
classData.name: parseBool

classData.comment:
/**
     * Decode env value as a finite number.
     *
     * Note: `Number(undefined)` returns `NaN` (not undefined), and `NaN ?? fallback` does
     * NOT fire `??` (only fires on nullish). The undefined-return for absent input is
     * load-bearing so a nullish-coalescing env-override fallback fires (a `NaN` would not).
     *
     * @param {String} envVarName
     * @param {Object} [opts]
     * @param {Object} [opts.env=process.env]
     * @param {Function} [opts.warn=console.warn]
     * @returns {Number|undefined}
     */
classData.description:
Decode env value as a finite number.

Note: Number(undefined) returns NaN (not undefined), and NaN ?? fallback does NOT fire ?? (only fires on nullish). The undefined-return for absent input is load-bearing so a nullish-coalescing env-override fallback fires (a NaN would not).
classData.name: parseNumber

classData.comment:
/**
     * Decode provider keep-alive values.
     *
     * Numeric tokens stay numeric so `-1` (keep resident) and `0` (unload after
     * request) survive env binding without becoming opaque strings. Duration-style
     * tokens such as `10m` or `1h` pass through unchanged for providers that accept
     * human-readable retention windows.
     *
     * @param {String} envVarName
     * @param {Object} [opts]
     * @param {Object} [opts.env=process.env]
     * @returns {Number|String|undefined}
     */
classData.description:
Decode provider keep-alive values.

Numeric tokens stay numeric so -1 (keep resident) and 0 (unload after request) survive env binding without becoming opaque strings. Duration-style tokens such as 10m or 1h pass through unchanged for providers that accept human-readable retention windows.
classData.name: parseKeepAlive

classData.comment:
/**
     * Decode env value as a comma-separated string list.
     *
     * Empty items are ignored so operators can format values as
     * `client-a, client-b` without introducing blank allowlist entries.
     *
     * @param {String} envVarName
     * @param {Object} [opts]
     * @param {Object} [opts.env=process.env]
     * @returns {String[]|undefined}
     */
classData.description:
Decode env value as a comma-separated string list.

Empty items are ignored so operators can format values as client-a, client-b without introducing blank allowlist entries.
classData.name: parseCsv

classData.comment:
/**
     * Decode env value as port (integer 1..65535).
     * @param {String} envVarName
     * @param {Object} [opts]
     * @param {Object} [opts.env=process.env]
     * @param {Function} [opts.warn=console.warn]
     * @returns {Number|undefined}
     */
classData.name: parsePort

classData.comment:
/**
     * Identity passthrough — reads `env[envVarName]` and returns the raw string (or
     * undefined if absent/empty). Signals "we explicitly want the raw string".
     * @param {String} envVarName
     * @param {Object} [opts]
     * @param {Object} [opts.env=process.env]
     * @returns {String|undefined}
     */
classData.description: Identity passthrough — reads env[envVarName] and returns the raw string (or undefined if absent/empty). Signals "we explicitly want the raw string".
classData.name: parseString

classData.comment:
/**
     * Decode env value as URL, normalized (trailing slash stripped).
     * @param {String} envVarName
     * @param {Object} [opts]
     * @param {Object} [opts.env=process.env]
     * @param {Function} [opts.warn=console.warn]
     * @returns {String|undefined}
     */
classData.description: Decode env value as URL, normalized (trailing slash stripped).
classData.name: parseUrl
